name: Advancing AI-Resilient Early-Career Pathways Initiative
description: Initiative to identify and pilot innovative models helping early-career workers thrive in an AI-transformed labour market. Awards $50k–$100k per grant (3–6 grants anticipated) for an 8-month pilot (April–November 2026). Eligible applicants include workforce development orgs, training providers, employer-led entities, and for-profit organisations.
notes: Deadline passed (25 Feb 2026). Required in-person convening 25 March 2026. Monitor JFF for next round.

name: LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026: Global AI Workforce Grant
description: Grant programme supporting nonprofit organisations preparing young adults for an AI-powered labour market through AI literacy, digital skills, and employment pathways. Grants range from $200k–$300k; focus countries include US, UK, France, Germany, and India. Open to legally registered nonprofits with a workforce development mandate.
eligible_regions: global (US, UK, France, Germany, India priority)
notes: Deadline was 15 March 2026 — may have just closed. Verify next cycle. Restricted to nonprofits, not think tanks directly.

name: Mozilla Foundation Democracy x AI Cohort 2026
description: Incubator programme funding ~10 projects building AI-driven tools that strengthen democracy. Tier I: $50k + 12-month incubation; Tier II (Sustain): up to $250k additional. Focus on information integrity, institutional transparency, and civic participation. Applicants must have a working prototype.
amount_notes: Tier I: $50k; Tier II additional up to $250k (total ~$300k)
notes: Initial proposal deadline 16 March 2026; full proposals invited April 15. Requires working prototype and open-source commitment. Civic/democracy focus, not labour markets.

name: Alfred P. Sloan Foundation — Economic Research on Returns to R&D
description: Consortium of funders led by Sloan and Coefficient Giving seeking empirical research on social and economic returns to R&D investment. Awards up to $250k. LOI due 30 April 2026; invitation-only full proposals follow. Tangential fit for teams studying AI investment and labour productivity.
notes: LOI deadline 30 April 2026. Full proposals by invitation only. Focus is on R&D return estimates, not future-of-work specifically.

name: William T. Grant Foundation — Research Grants on Reducing Inequality 2026
description: Grants up to $600k funding research to build, test, or increase understanding of programmes and policies that reduce inequality in youth outcomes (ages 5–25) in the US. Can encompass AI-driven labour market inequality affecting young workers. Second LOI cycle due July 2026.
notes: LOI due 29 July 2026 (second cycle). Focus strictly on ages 5–25; best fit if research targets young workers entering AI-affected labour markets.

name: Washington Center for Equitable Growth — AI Workers & Competition RFP
description: Research grants studying how AI, automation, algorithmic management, and electronic surveillance affect US workers, wages, and market competition. Awards $25k–$80k over up to 2 years. Open to individual researchers and teams at think tanks, universities, and research institutions.
amount_notes: $25,000–$80,000; slightly larger grants possible if well-justified
notes: Most recent cycle closed Feb 2025; next RFP likely late 2026. Excellent thematic fit for think tank teams. Monitor equitablegrowth.org.

name: GitLab Foundation AI for Economic Opportunity Fund
description: Demonstration grants to US-based nonprofits using AI to measurably improve economic mobility for low-income populations. Each award is $250k plus OpenAI API credits and 6 months of technical support. The 2026 cohort (cycle 4) has already been announced; next cycle TBC.
amount_notes: $250,000 per org plus OpenAI API credits and cohort support
notes: 2026 cohort (16 orgs) announced March 5, 2026. Next open call TBC. Open to nonprofits, not think tanks directly unless nonprofit-registered.

name: Humanity AI — Pooled Philanthropic Fund (Labor & Economy Track)
organization: Humanity AI (Ford, MacArthur, Mellon, Omidyar, Mozilla, Packard, Kapor, Doris Duke, Lumina, Siegel Family Endowment)
description: A $500M, 5-year pooled fund from 10 leading foundations dedicated to people-centred AI development. The 'Labor & Economy' track focuses on equipping workers to thrive amid AI-driven transformation. Grants expected to flow in 2026; application details pending. Highest possible thematic alignment for think tank AI + future-of-work research.
amount_notes: $500M total fund; per-grant amounts TBC when applications open
notes: Application details and per-grant amounts not yet announced. Sign up for updates at humanityai.ai. Managed by Rockefeller Philanthropy Advisors.

name: Cooperative AI Foundation Research Grants
description: Research grants on AI cooperation, including understanding and evaluating cooperation-relevant capabilities. No fixed upper limit; cost-effectiveness is weighted. Priority areas include understanding AI cooperation propensities and multi-stakeholder AI governance relevant to labour and economic settings.
amount_notes: No fixed ceiling; applicants encouraged to be ambitious
notes: Rolling applications. Thematic overlap is indirect; strongest fit if research addresses cooperation in human-AI work systems.

name: Amazon Research Awards — Fairness in AI & Human-Computer Interaction
description: Unrestricted research gifts to academic institutions in areas including fairness in AI, knowledge management, NLP, and HCI. No IP rights retained by Amazon. Rolling calls; typically $50k–$150k per award. Relevant for think tanks affiliated with universities or structured as research institutions.
notes: Requires institutional affiliation (university or nonprofit research org). Applications reviewed in rolling cycles; check portal for current open areas.

name: Google.org AI Opportunity Fund — US Workforce Development
description: Part of a $75M commitment to help over 1 million Americans learn AI skills through workforce development and education organisations. Focus on rural/under-served workers, public sector employees, small businesses, and nonprofits. Grants to best-in-class workforce orgs; not an open call but accepts relationship-based inquiries.
amount_notes: $75M total programme; individual grants vary; $15M sub-tranche for public sector recently announced
notes: Not an open RFP — Google.org selects partners proactively. Engage through introductions or foundation relations. Excellent fit for AI workforce research with practical outputs.

name: Siegel Family Endowment — AI & Workforce Research Grants
description: Siegel Family Endowment investing $15.4M+ in grants focused on how workers shape AI's transformation of work. Supports research and programmes examining hiring, management, monitoring of workers, and evidence-based policies on worker data rights and AI impact. 2025–2026 cohort of Siegel Research Fellows announced.
amount_notes: $15.4M total announced; individual award amounts TBC
notes: Siegel is a Humanity AI coalition member with an active research fellowship programme. Explicitly focused on worker agency in AI transformation. Contact directly for 2026 grant deadlines.

name: IBM Impact Accelerator — Education & Workforce Development Cohort
description: Global RFP for nonprofits and research organisations to develop AI solutions for education and workforce development. Selected organisations (5 per cohort) receive two-year pro bono access to IBM watsonx, Granite AI models, IBM Cloud, and implementation support from IBM researchers and consultants.
amount_notes: Pro bono technology package; in-kind support including IBM tools and consulting (no cash grant)
notes: Deadline 25 March 2026 — apply immediately. Excellent fit for think tanks working on AI and workforce research. Global scope. Contact: impact.accelerator@ibm.com

name: Knight Foundation — Ethics and Governance of AI Fund
description: Ongoing initiative supporting civil society organisations working on AI ethics, governance, and public interest. The Foundation has committed $7.6M to nine initial grantees; supports research on governance, norms, values, and responsible AI systems.
amount_notes: $7.6M committed across nine grantees; individual award sizes not published
notes: Rolling applications. Good fit for AI governance and policy research. Check knightfoundation.org for 2026 RFP details. May need to align with Knight's democracy and civics mission.
